div1.entryFree:
Audible, 1) so as to be easily heard, loud; adverbially: the very mercy of the law cries out most a. Meas. V, 413.
2) capable of hearing, attentive, opposed to deaf: let me have war; it's spritely walking, a., and full of vent. Peace is a very apoplexy, mulled, deaf, sleepy, insensible, Cor. IV, 5, 238.
